questions.question_text: Who is the speaker in the poem? Answer in the context of The Shed.
questions.question_html: Who is the speaker in the poem? Answer in the context of The Shed.

solutions.text: The speaker of the poem is poet Frank Flynn when he was a small boy. The poem is about the speaker's (child's) fear and curiosity about the Shed which is at the end of his garden.
solutions.html: The speaker of the poem is poet Frank Flynn when he was a small boy. The poem is about the speaker's (child's) fear and curiosity about the Shed which is at the end of his garden.

questions.question_text: Is she/he afraid or curious, or both? Answer in the context of 'The Shed'.
questions.question_html: Is she/he afraid or curious, or both? Answer in the context of 'The Shed'.

solutions.text:
The speaker seems to be both curious as well as afraid to go inside the shed. He wants to find out what is inside the shed.
At the same time, he is afraid because he thinks that strange voices could be heard from here and there is possibly a ghost who lives inside the shed.
solutions.html:
The speaker seems to be both curious as well as afraid to go inside the shed. He wants to find out what is inside the shed.
At the same time, he is afraid because he thinks that strange voices could be heard from here and there is possibly a ghost who lives inside the shed.

questions.question_text: What is she/he planning to do soon? Answer in the context of 'The Shed'.
questions.question_html: What is she/he planning to do soon? Answer in the context of 'The Shed'.

solutions.text: She/he is planning to go inside the shed soon.
solutions.html: She/he is planning to go inside the shed soon.

questions.question_text: But not just yet... suggests doubt, fear, hesitation, laziness or something else. Choose the word which seems right to you. Tell others why you chose it. (The shed)
questions.question_html: But not just yet... suggests doubt, fear, hesitation, laziness or something else. Choose the word which seems right to you. Tell others why you chose it. (The shed)

solutions.text: "But not just yet..." seems to suggest a lack of preparation. The speaker wants to be further certain and prepare himself for this endeavour. He has already overcome his fears, so there is no fear. However, waiting for the right time could be a reason for postponing the act.
solutions.html: "But not just yet..." seems to suggest a lack of preparation. The speaker wants to be further certain and prepare himself for this endeavour. He has already overcome his fears, so there is no fear. However, waiting for the right time could be a reason for postponing the act.

questions.question_text: Is there a room in your house or a house in your neighbourhood/locality where you would rather not go alone, and never at night? If there is such a place and a story to go with it, let others hear all about it. (The shed)
questions.question_html: Is there a room in your house or a house in your neighbourhood/locality where you would rather not go alone, and never at night? If there is such a place and a story to go with it, let others hear all about it. (The shed)

solutions.text:
There is a storeroom in my house which is in the backyard of the house. It is a little away from the main structure. It is full of odd objects and not even lit properly. Thus, going to the storeroom, at night, needs a lot of courage. My mother once asked me to fetch certain things from the storeroom.
When I entered the storeroom, I felt as if I saw the movement of shadows. Screamed with terror, but later got to know that those shadows were nothing but my mind's imagination.
solutions.html:
There is a storeroom in my house which is in the backyard of the house. It is a little away from the main structure. It is full of odd objects and not even lit properly. Thus, going to the storeroom, at night, needs a lot of courage. My mother once asked me to fetch certain things from the storeroom.
When I entered the storeroom, I felt as if I saw the movement of shadows. Screamed with terror, but later got to know that those shadows were nothing but my mind's imagination.
